target_semantics: Expose read-only project, file-search, file-read, and symbol-reading tools to agentic LLM presets.

target_semantics: Tune garbage collection thresholds after startup to reduce Emacs pauses.

target_semantics: Load the current direnv environment when entering a project.
divergence: Selected opens provision the destination environment for initial mode hooks, but direct background find-file-buffer loads deliberately do not retarget Lem. Environment mutation and rollback are process-global and sequential rather than atomic, so existing processes are unchanged while worker threads can observe an intermediate multi-variable state or whichever buffer environment is active at launch.
notes: The implementation keeps startup-cached WORKDIR in workspace.lisp separate, tracks exact directories rather than project roots, matches derived process modes and explicitly marked process buffers, requires explicit direnv-allow, never auto-authorizes .envrc, propagates PATH to future subprocesses, preserves defvar preferences across reload, bounds synchronous calls at 300 seconds and each streamed output at 4 MiB, prevalidates JSON before sequential mutation, accepts successful empty export output as no changes, and retains/displays no stderr values. The real ncurses gate covers mode-hook and child-process inheritance, argv safety, caches/reload, nested and outside transitions, non-file scope, blocked/allow/update behavior, empty output, timeout, malformed output, and recovery.

target_semantics: Transparently decrypt and re-encrypt SOPS-managed files while editing.
divergence: Existing local YAML, JSON, dotenv, INI, and text files are transparent, but Lem lacks sops-mode's guided encrypted-file creation command, input-type overrides, and file-notification auto-revert.
notes: The real ncurses gate proves plaintext open with ciphertext-only save, formatter inhibition, disk and buffer preservation across encryption failure, stderr censorship, SOPS-aware external revert, read-only decrypt failure and retry, plaintext prefilter fall-through, and reload idempotence. Synchronous calls are bounded at 300 seconds and 64 MiB output; persistence tracks ciphertext identity instead of comparing it with the intentionally decrypted buffer.

target_semantics: Edit grep matches and persist the changes back to their source files.
divergence: Edits write through immediately to source buffers instead of entering wgrep's explicit staged edit mode with finish and abort commands; persistence uses each source buffer's ordinary save workflow.
notes: The real ncurses gate inserts text in Vi state, proves the result and source buffer change while disk does not, saves through the source preview and proves disk persistence, then verifies Escape returns Insert to Normal without dismissing results. Point-preserving source edits, one owned preview timer, teardown invalidation, and hook-free background preview switching prevent the prior crash and Vi-state loss.

target_semantics: Describe commands, variables, keys, and bindings from the leader help prefix.
divergence: Callable and variable selection now matches the active Helpful workflows, but the rendered help lacks Helpful's source links, references, callers, and richer cross-reference navigation; key and binding help retain Lem's core presentation.
notes: The real ncurses gate invokes SPC h k/v, proves non-command callable coverage, typed display-only metadata, exact package-qualified selection, bounded secret-safe values, and reload behavior. SPC h K and SPC h b remain source-verified core help bindings.

target_semantics: Honor EditorConfig rules in programming buffers.
divergence: Only the documented core property mapping is applied, and charset changes occur after initial decoding and affect subsequent writes only; arbitrary EditorConfig plugin behavior is not reproduced.
notes: The official CLI supplies hierarchy, root, and unset semantics for every steady-state local file buffer; the TUI covers indentation, LF/CR/CRLF, subsequent-write Latin-1, trailing whitespace, and final-newline behavior, while max_line_length mapping remains source-verified.

target_semantics: Start quietly and quickly without toolbars, menus, startup text, or configuration compilation noise.
divergence: Emacs' temporary GC and file-handler tuning has no exact SBCL analogue; Lem starts a terminal image rather than an Emacs GUI or daemon frame.
notes: The installed-wrapper ncurses gate proves a complete AOT-backed cold boot within 30 seconds, zero runtime configuration FASLs, writable cached logging, no process-stream errors or compiler chatter, and an empty Org *scratch* instead of Lem's welcome page. It then enforces a 10-second repeated-start budget and preserves an explicit --log-filename override.

target_semantics: Start a reusable editor server and point EDITOR, VISUAL, and GIT_EDITOR at its client.
divergence: One authoritative ncurses pane is reused instead of a headless daemon or graphical/new-terminal frames; arbitrary evaluation is absent, parent shells must export editor variables separately, and an unavailable reusable pane falls back to a fresh Lem.
notes: The owner-private bounded protocol supports positioned blocking/no-wait multi-file opens, current-buffer attachment, save/clean finish, recoverable abort, tmux handoff, live-socket reuse, hostile metadata refusal, partial-start rollback, and clean owned-artifact removal. Successful startup sets GIT_EDITOR and fills otherwise-unset VISUAL/EDITOR for editor child processes.

notes: SPC p g converts Emacs regexp syntax, passes it after --, searches the exact tracked-plus-untracked project file set in bounded argv batches, and renders editable peek results off the editor thread.

target_semantics: Automatically revert changed files and persist places, prompt history, the kill ring, and searches.
divergence: Command-triggered polling replaces Emacs idle timers and filesystem notifications; same-metadata rewrites above 16 MiB are detected on explicit save but not periodic scans; transactional reload snapshots are capped at 64 MiB; saved places cover local files but not directory buffers; non-file buffers require explicit adapters; prompt persistence is a reviewed default-deny subset; oversized entries remain live but are omitted; stale processes can resurrect cleared histories.
notes: The 45-check ncurses gate covers safe clean and dirty external changes, large/deleted/new-file stale-save guards, Save As races, modified quit refusal, fresh-process file positions, separate literal/regexp history, prompt privacy/live caps, physical Vi-aware kill order, bounded non-evaluating state reads, private modes, failure-safe exit, and stale concurrent-process merging. Recent files remain split out under MISC-013.

target_semantics: Compile configuration ahead of time and avoid writing compiled artifacts into the source tree.
divergence: none
notes: Nix compiles all 87 ASDF components into an immutable FASL output before constructing the installed wrapper. A cold installed-wrapper launch reports that output, loads the complete configuration, and writes no FASLs into an empty user cache; direct development loads without an existing ASDF policy install a source-scoped XDG cache translation before loading the system.

target_semantics: Persist a deduplicated MRU of at most 300 recent files across editor processes and open it with M-g r.

target_semantics: Automatically persist modified bookmarks at exit and restore them in a fresh editor process.
divergence: none
notes: SPC b m and SPC RET retain Lem's native bookmark UI. Bookmark triples are bounded and validated inside the private non-evaluating state format, written atomically at clean exit, restored at startup, and reconciled by a baseline-aware three-way merge so stale processes preserve independent additions while local same-name updates and deletions win conflicts. The ncurses gate covers creation, point-accurate fresh-process jumps, concurrent mutation, reload idempotence, and malicious reader syntax.
